title: REMEDi4ALL - Standard Operating Procedure - Creating, Distributing and Updating SOPs
author: Witt, Gesa
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: An       SOP       serves to standardize       work flows       in       a laboratory       and to assure       consistent       documentation.    In most cases it is applied to recurring procedures.   Before an SOP can be created the process itself needs to be finalized (e.g. an assay is optimized, best cell culture conditions are determined or the optimal use of a device is found). All required and useful information should be collected and presented in a way that it can be understood easily and enables the reader to operate independently on the basis of the document   . For this purpose the procedures       are described       accurately. Potential sources of failure can be       highlighted within the document if useful.
